_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart/LeftBehind development and eval.

procedure.name: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) with esophageal dilation
procedure.indication: Recurrent solid-food dysphagia from known distal peptic esophageal stricture (last dilated to 15 mm May 2025)
procedure.prep_quality: NPO since midnight — adequate for upper endoscopy

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: EGD with esophageal dilation. INDICATION: recurrent solid-food dysphagia, known distal peptic esophageal stricture. SEDATION: MAC/propofol. PERIPROCEDURAL ANTICOAGULATION: apixaban 2.5 mg BID (corrected dose) held x 3 days pre-procedure per current CrCl obtained before scheduling; daily aspirin 81 mg held x 5 days in coordination with cardiology. Last apixaban dose documented prior to hold. FINDINGS: The esophagus demonstrated a smooth, tapered narrowing in the distal esophagus consistent with recurrent peptic stricture, luminal diameter approximately 10-11 mm, not comfortably traversable with the standard adult gastroscope on first pass. Mild distal esophagitis, LA grade A. Savary-Gilliard bougie dilation was performed in graded fashion to 15 mm with good effect and a positive dilation sign; the scope was subsequently passed easily. No mucosal tear beyond expected dilation effect, minimal self-limited oozing, no active bleeding at completion. The stomach and examined duodenum to the second portion were normal. Mucosal biopsies were obtained from the distal esophagus/stricture (specimen A) to exclude eosinophilic esophagitis and malignancy given recurrence, and to sample the LA grade A esophagitis. IMPRESSION: Recurrent distal peptic esophageal stricture, dilated to 15 mm with good result; LA grade A esophagitis. No malignancy grossly. Biopsies pending. No immediate complications.

findings.finding: Recurrent distal peptic esophageal stricture, ~10-11 mm luminal diameter
findings.action_taken: Savary-Gilliard bougie dilation to 15 mm, good effect

findings.finding: Normal stomach and duodenum to D2

specimens.site: distal esophagus / stricture (includes LA grade A esophagitis mucosa)

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Post-dilation and post-biopsy precautions reviewed. Soft diet advanced as tolerated. Return precautions for chest pain, fever, odynophagia, inability to swallow saliva, hematemesis, or melena.

followup.recommendation: Review distal esophageal biopsy pathology (r/o EoE / malignancy); call patient with result and document plan
Resume apixaban 2.5 mg BID and aspirin 81 mg per cardiology-coordinated plan once post-dilation bleeding risk acceptable (typically 24-48 h if no bleeding)
Continue omeprazole 40 mg every morning; repeat EGD with dilation as needed for recurrent dysphagia

planted_followup_items.description: Distal esophageal/stricture biopsy pending to exclude eosinophilic esophagitis and malignancy given recurrent stricture; result must be communicated and drive further management
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with result, document EoE/malignancy exclusion and next steps

planted_followup_items.description: Resume held apixaban 2.5 mg BID (corrected reduced dose) after higher-bleed-risk dilation, coordinated with cardiology and keyed to bleeding risk; on a DOAC so resumption timing is safety-critical
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm and document safe apixaban resumption date/time with cardiology

planted_followup_items.description: Resume held aspirin 81 mg after dilation per cardiology plan; additive bleed risk was the reason for hold
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm aspirin resumption timing with cardiology

planted_followup_items.description: Recurrent peptic stricture pattern (second symptomatic recurrence) — establish repeat dilation / EGD plan and PPI optimization for recurrent dysphagia
planted_followup_items.expected_action: set PRN repeat-dilation plan and reinforce high-dose PPI adherence

planted_followup_items.description: Post-dilation perforation/bleeding watch, heightened by periprocedural DOAC and antiplatelet exposure
planted_followup_items.expected_action: monitor for chest pain, fever, odynophagia, hematemesis, melena; act if positive

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored. SYNTHETIC.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels scratchy and swallowing is a little sore today, but water and soft food are going down fine.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD/post-dilation sore throat and transient odynophagia; tolerating liquids and soft diet, no obstruction
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and swallowing tenderness are expected after scope + dilation and settle over 24-72h. Advise soft diet, fluids, and return precautions for inability to swallow saliva, chest pain, or fever.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-dilation symptoms with preserved swallowing; no red flag for perforation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear about the biopsy from my esophagus, and when is it safe for me to start my apixaban and baby aspirin again?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending pathology and anticoagulant/antiplatelet resumption timing
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: biopsies pending (~day 5, expected 2026-08-11); team will call with result and plan. Confirm apixaban 2.5 mg BID and aspirin 81 mg resumption timing per the cardiology-coordinated plan (typically 24-48h if no bleeding).
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to pending_pathology (F1) and the med_resume items (F2/F3).

contacts.verbatim: I feel a little bloated and burpy since the test, but the swallowing is already better than before — meat went down okay tonight.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy insufflation bloating; improving dysphagia consistent with successful dilation
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; bloating/gas from insufflation is self-limited, and improved solid-food swallowing reflects a good dilation result. Reinforce omeprazole adherence and return precautions.
contacts.rationale: Benign insufflation symptom with clinical improvement; no escalation warranted.
